title: Luma acutiflora
intro: Luma acutiflora, also known as Chilean lavender or luma, is a flowering plant native to South America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality due to its calming and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Luma acutiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly linalool and geraniol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Luma acutiflora is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oil infusions, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and improve mood.
taxonomy: Luma acutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and South America. Luma acutiflora is often classified as a shrub or small tree, belonging to the genus Luma. Within the Verbenaceae family, it is part of the order Lamiales. The plant's classification is also sometimes considered within the larger family Lamiaceae, although this classification is less commonly used.
uses: Luma acutiflora, also known as the white cloud, is a flowering plant with potential medicinal properties.
